# sha1

```go
import "crypto/sha1"
```

Package sha1 implements the SHA\-1 hash algorithm as defined in RFC 3174.

SHA\-1 is cryptographically broken and should not be used for secure applications.

## Index

- [Constants](<#constants>)
- [func New\(\) hash.Hash](<#New>)
- [func Sum\(data \[\]byte\) \[Size\]byte](<#Sum>)

## Constants

<a name="BlockSize"></a>The blocksize of SHA\-1 in bytes.

```go
const BlockSize = 64
```

<a name="Size"></a>The size of a SHA\-1 checksum in bytes.

```go
const Size = 20
```

<a name="New"></a>

## func [New](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/sha1/sha1.go#L115>)

```go
func New() hash.Hash
```

New returns a new [hash.Hash](<https://pkg.go.dev/hash/#Hash>) computing the SHA1 checksum. The Hash also implements [encoding.BinaryMarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryMarshaler>), [encoding.BinaryAppender](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryAppender>) and [encoding.BinaryUnmarshaler](<https://pkg.go.dev/encoding/#BinaryUnmarshaler>) to marshal and unmarshal the internal state of the hash.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/sha1"
	"fmt"
	"io"
)

func main() {
	h := sha1.New()
	io.WriteString(h, "His money is twice tainted:")
	io.WriteString(h, " 'taint yours and 'taint mine.")
	fmt.Printf("% x", h.Sum(nil))
}
```

#### Output

```go
59 7f 6a 54 00 10 f9 4c 15 d7 18 06 a9 9a 2c 87 10 e7 47 bd
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (File)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/sha1"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"log"
	"os"
)

func main() {
	f, err := os.Open("file.txt")
	if err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}
	defer f.Close()

	h := sha1.New()
	if _, err := io.Copy(h, f); err != nil {
		log.Fatal(err)
	}

	fmt.Printf("% x", h.Sum(nil))
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Sum"></a>

## func [Sum](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/crypto/sha1/sha1.go#L273>)

```go
func Sum(data []byte) [Size]byte
```

Sum returns the SHA\-1 checksum of the data.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"crypto/sha1"
	"fmt"
)

func main() {
	data := []byte("This page intentionally left blank.")
	fmt.Printf("% x", sha1.Sum(data))
}
```

#### Output

```go
af 06 49 23 bb f2 30 15 96 aa c4 c2 73 ba 32 17 8e bc 4a 96
```

</p>
</details>
